text: **Anna:** You know, I am from the {country|田舎/農村|/ˈkʌntri/|I live in the country.} and sometimes I {miss|恋しく思う/懐かしむ|/mɪs/|I miss my hometown.} it.
**Anna:** But I really like life in the {city|都市/街|/ˈsɪti/|I love living in the city.}!
**Anna:** I love the city.
**Anna:** Oh look!
**Anna:** Someone lost a {bag|かばん/バッグ|/bæɡ/|I found a bag on the street.}
**Anna:** Maybe it's theirs.
**Anna:** Excuse me.
**Anna:** Is this bag yours?
**Sarah:** No, it's not mine.
**Sarah:** It might be hers.
**Anna:** Thank you
**Anna:** Excuse me, is this bag yours?

**Phil:** Yes, that's mine.
**Anna:** These are all my {travel|旅行の/移動の|/ˈtrævəl/|I love to travel around the world.} things.
**Anna:** Thank you.
**Anna:** Are you okay?
**Anna:** You seem {nervous|緊張した/不安な|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|He looks nervous before the test.}
**Phil:** Well, this is my first {visit|訪問/来訪|/ˈvɪzɪt/|We will visit the museum.} to Washington DC.
**Phil:** I'm from a small town in the country.
**Phil:** Feel a little {lost|迷った/道に迷った|/lɔːst/|I am lost in this big city.}
**Phil:** I'm from the country too.
**Anna:** And I understand.
**Anna:** When I first came here, I felt lost.
**Anna:** All the time.
**Phil:** So do you like living in the country or in the city?
**Anna:** I like to live in the {city|都市/街|/ˈsɪti/|I live in a big city}.
**Phil:** Why?
**Anna:** The city is {exciting|わくわくする/刺激的な|/ɪkˈstaɪdɪŋ/|An exciting movie is fun}.
**Anna:** It has more {culture|文化|/ˈkʌltʃər/|Japanese culture is interesting} than the country.
**Anna:** There are many {museums|博物館|/mjuˈziəmz/|Museums show history} and {restaurants|レストラン|/ˈrestrɒnts/|Restaurants serve good food}.
**Anna:** Every night there's {theater|劇場|/ˈθɪətər/|Theater shows are exciting} and music
**Anna:** And there are more {jobs|仕事|/dʒɒbz/|Good jobs pay well}.
**Anna:** That is why I'm here.
**Anna:** Well, I agree.
**Anna:** There is more culture in the city and there might be more jobs, but the country has more {nature|自然|/ˈneɪtʃər/|Nature is beautiful}.
**Anna:** It's {peaceful|平和な/静かな|/ˈpiːsfəl/|A peaceful place is calm} and beautiful.
**Anna:** There are trees and {mountains|山|/ˈmaʊntənz/|Mountains are tall}.
**Phil:** The air is clean.
**Anna:** You can go {hiking|ハイキング|/ˈhaɪkɪŋ/|Hiking is good exercise} and {camping|キャンプ|/ˈkæmpɪŋ/|Camping is fun}.
**Anna:** The city is not beautiful.
**Anna:** It's {noisy|うるさい/騒々しい|/ˈnɔɪzi/|A noisy room is loud} and dirty.
**Anna:** I disagree.
**Anna:** I think all the different {buildings|建物|/ˈbɪldɪŋz/|Buildings are tall} are beautiful.
**Anna:** And I like to watch all the different people.
**Phil:** That's another thing that is different.
**Phil:** People in the country are {friendly|フレンドリーな/親切な|/ˈfrendli/|Friendly people are kind}.
**Anna:** They always say hello.
**Anna:** Here, no one says hello.
**Anna:** I think city people are
**Anna:** {Rude|無礼な/失礼な|/ruːd/|Rude people are not polite}.
**Anna:** Well I agree.
**Anna:** Country people friendly.
**Anna:** But I don't think city people are rude.
**Anna:** I think they're just {busy|忙しい|/ˈbɪzi/|Busy people work a lot}.
**Phil:** That's a good point.
**Anna:** Look at me.
**Anna:** I {live|住む} in the {city|都市/街} and I said hello to you.
**Anna:** But you are from the {country|田舎/農村}.
**Anna:** I have an {idea|アイデア/考え}.
**Anna:** Let's say {hello|挨拶する} to people.
**Anna:** To many people.
**Phil:** What?
**Phil:** Why?
**Anna:** Well, if we say hello, then they will say hello to other people.
**Anna:** Hello?
**Phil:** And they will say hello to more people.
**Phil:** That's a great {idea|素晴らしいアイデア}.
**Anna:** I'm glad you {found|見つけた/発見した} my bag.
**Anna:** Come on, let's go say hello to people
**Anna:** We don't have to {agree|同意する} with people.
**Anna:** They have their {opinions|意見/考え}, we have ours.
**Anna:** And as we like to say, you can always {agree|同意する} to {disagree|意見が合わない}.
**Anna:** Until next time.
